content: Hey. I’m porting over a network driver into rust and need SkBuff support. I’ve seen wrappers on the legacy documentation (https://rust-for-linux.github.io/docs/kernel/net/struct.SkBuff.html) , but in the new one it vanished (https://rust.docs.kernel.org/kernel/?search=SkBuff). Can someone give me some more context in the matter?

If you mean general context, https://rust-for-linux.com/branches#rust.

Otherwise, Cc'ing @Trevor Gross @Valentin Obst @FUJITA Tomonori.
I just want to where the project is on that front. Is there a bind/implementation ready for usage?
Also, wanted a status check on NAPI as well
I don’t think anyone is currently working on the bindings needed for NIC drivers.

Doing it for a PCI card. I’ve seen that PCI integration is already mature. Maybe I can contribute with it (Talking about the rest of the bindings necessary to make the NIC driver)… I’m doing a university project on the matter.
The abstractions for NIC drivers don’t seem likely to be merged into mainline, so I doubt anyone would maintain them.
Not even socket buffers on their own?
Maybe that the reason why it was present in the legacy documentation and are not there anymore
As far as I know, no one is implementing a driver that requires  skbuff abstractions and has a chance of being merged into mainline.
Guilherme Lima has marked this topic as resolved.
